name: Pot Roast with Dill Gravy
recipeIngredient: 4 lb Beef chuck arm pot roast (boneless)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a Dutch oven or large pan with a tight-fitting cover, brown the meat in fat. Season with salt and pepper, then remove from the pan. Pour off the fat drippings.
2. Return the meat to the pan and sprinkle with dill seed. Place onion slices on top.
3. Cover and simmer for 2 1/2 to 3 hours, or until done. Alternatively, cook in a 325°F oven for the same amount of time.
4. When ready, remove meat and onions to a platter and keep warm.
5. For 2 cups of gravy, measure liquid from the pan into a 2-cup measuring cup. Let stand for 1 minute to allow fat to rise to the top. Discard all but 4 tablespoons (or less) of fat.
6. Add enough water (or other liquid) to measure 1 1/2 cups of liquid. Return to the pan.
7. In a separate cup, mix 1/2 cup cold water with flour. Add this mixture slowly to the liquid in the pan while stirring constantly. Bring to a boil and cook until thickened, about 3 minutes.
8. Remove the pan from heat. Add sour cream, a small amount at a time, and mix well. Taste gravy and correct seasoning if necessary with salt and pepper.
9. If gravy is not hot enough when ready to serve, place the pan over very low heat (do not boil), but do not worry if curdling occurs as it does not affect flavor. Serve gravy separately.
10. Slice meat and serve.
